pages.raw_text:
(By United Preas Leased Wire.)
RATHDRUM. Idaho, Aug. 22.--
Cut off from the. outer world by


complete and overwhelming de.
struction of Northern Pacific rail.
road bridges, unable to reach sur.
rounding towns by telephone or


telegraph, with Athol, Granite, Sew
erns, Drywood and other small
towns reported DESTROYED and doz-
ems of ranches an1 buildings In


ashes. the entire intervening coun.
try between Rathdrum and Spirit
Lake, apparently a seething, wind
swept furnace: the situation here


has assumed tragic aspect and
while his town itself Is in no inn
mediate danger a pail OF gloom
more nlack and heavy thaTathe sin.
isterfcinder-laden clouds of smoke,
iobsfuring the sun, shroads the
town


Since distress calls we're re-
ceited from many surrounding
towans Saturday night and frenzied
voices excitedly declared that


Athol and other towns could got


last an hour longer. wires have


been down in all directions and a


dozen villages os completely
isolated from communication with
the outer world as though the tele-


phone and telegraph never had


been invented


Before the wires snapped Satur-


day night. Granite, Idaho, reported
that the flames completely sur


rounded the town. Five buildings
in Athol had been destroyed and
the rest were bound to 60 at the
last report; and Severns, seeming
ly the center of the seething mass
of flames, is total loss. Ranches
between here and Spirit Lake have
been entirely consumed and the
damage between here and Sand
point has been heavy.


The CoEbin ranch, at last re-
ports, was in serious danger and


Bayview, near Athol. is said to
have been burned Towards Rim
Rock a terrible fire was leaping
over its course Of death and devas-
tationy Sunday and the town of Dry
Wood was threatened. The Mad
ison mill. near Dry Wood, was re-
duced to ashes. Great damage was
done at Echo beach. on Fish lake,
| and j. w. Smith, the county as
ssessor. has lost vast quantity of


standing timber.


Northern Pacific ties between


Rathdrum and Spokane have been
burned and twisted, smoke-grimed


rails are resting upon the roadbed,
with no support other than gravel


and earth.
